meta.description: The canonical scenario list. Each app's tests and recipes reference these by id, so 'payment-unknown-outcome' means the same thing in Kotlin, Dart and TypeScript. Flow scenarios cover the happy path per screen; failure scenarios cover the ways each screen is allowed to go wrong. If an app cannot demonstrate a scenario, that is a gap, not a detail.

flow.given: the events endpoint returns a page
flow.then: a cached list renders; pull-to-refresh refetches; server cache headers are respected
sectors, prices and availability render; sold-out sectors are visibly disabled

flow.given: sales are open and the room is busy
flow.then: the user joins the queue and sees a position
polling stops and the UI transitions to 'you are in'
sector and quantity (1..8) are pickable; controls disable when sold out
a hold is created with an Idempotency-Key and a live expiry countdown starts
an order is created; status shows pending and polling begins
the paid state renders with a confirmation affordance

failure.then: an Error state with a retry affordance; no crash, no silent swallow
a TimedOut state distinct from Error; retry available
automatic refresh with rotation; on refresh failure, global sign-out
respect Retry-After; back off polling; show a wait affordance
Conflict state; refresh availability and re-pick
countdown hits zero; hold is gone; recover by starting over
defensive parse rejects the payload; MalformedResponse state; never a crash
5xx maps to ServerError; retry available; request_id captured
two rapid taps produce one reservation; the Idempotency-Key makes the retry a no-op

payment.then: webhook confirms; polling stops on paid
declined maps to a failed state with a 'try another method' affordance
gateway timed out with unknown result; the app must NOT assume failure and must reconcile by polling; no double-charge, no false failure
network drops mid-request; the Idempotency-Key makes the retry safe; outcome resolved by polling
confirmation is late; the app keeps polling and does not give up prematurely
a refund transitions paid to refunded with its own UI
